name: Sauteed Veal Scallops in Wild Mushroom Cream Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound wild mushrooms (such as chanterelles, shiitake, oyster, or a combination)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the sauce: In a sauté pan set over moderately high heat, heat the oil and butter until hot. Add the shallots and cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Add the mushrooms, thyme, salt, and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 3-5 minutes, or until the mushrooms are soft. Add the garlic and cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Add the white wine and reduce by half. Add the veal demiglace and simmer for 5 minutes. Add the heavy cream and reduce until lightly thickened. Correct seasoning. Cover with a round of buttered wax paper and keep hot.
2. Make the veal: Dredge the scallops in flour, shaking off excess, and season with salt and pepper. In a large skillet set over moderate heat, heat the oil and butter until hot. Add the scallops and sauté for 1 minute on each side. Transfer to the sauce and cook for just a minute, seasoning the mixture with fresh lemon juice and chervil.
